rationale: Official Kering press release announcing licensing deal terms; factual corporate/strategic disclosure, not public opinion.
Independent trade press (WWD) cites analysts calling the beauty licence a 'structural bright spot', but explicitly flags core leather goods/RTW as still unresolved, capping the positive read.
Purely descriptive activation write-up of Monte Carlo campaign chapter two with no outlet evaluative verdict.
Collector forum debating durability of coated canvas vs classic canvas with no resolved consensus verdict; descriptive community discussion.
Explicitly split fan reception ('finally looking like luxury' vs 'feels very cheap and dated') with no dominant side; forced neutral per contested-opinion rule, also stale beyond freshness window.
Sell-side analyst recap frames Gucci as continuing 'drag' on Kering revenue, offset only by jewelry; negative momentum press, mild magnitude as routine quarterly checkpoint commentary.
Brand-owned Instagram promo content; self-praise is not public opinion, and engagement metrics were unreliable so left null.
